metadata.title: Regulation (EC) No 1901/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council — on medicinal products for paediatric use

rules.rule_text: A marketing authorisation application under Article 6 of Directive 2001/83/EC for a medicinal product not yet authorised in the Community shall be regarded as valid only if it includes one of the following: (a) results of all studies and details of all information collected in compliance with an agreed paediatric investigation plan; (b) a product-specific waiver; (c) a class waiver pursuant to Article 11; (d) a deferral.
rules.verbatim_quote: An application for marketing authorisation under Article 6 of Directive 2001/83/EC in respect of a medicinal product for human use which is not authorised in the Community at the time of entry into force of this Regulation shall be regarded as valid only if it includes, in addition to the particulars and documents referred to in Article 8(3) of Directive 2001/83/EC, one of the following: (a) the results of all studies performed and details of all information collected in compliance with an agreed paediatric investigation plan; (b) a decision of the Agency granting a product-specific waiver; (c) a decision of the Agency granting a class waiver pursuant to Article 11; (d) a decision of the Agency granting a deferral.
exceptions: Products authorised under Articles 10, 10a, 13 to 16 or 16a to 16i of Directive 2001/83/EC (Article 9)

rules.rule_text: The documents submitted pursuant to Article 7(1) shall, cumulatively, cover all subsets of the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: The documents submitted pursuant to paragraph 1 shall, cumulatively, cover all subsets of the paediatric population.

rules.rule_text: For authorised medicinal products protected by a supplementary protection certificate or a qualifying patent, the PIP requirement under Article 7 also applies to applications for new indications (including paediatric indications), new pharmaceutical forms, and new routes of administration.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of authorised medicinal products which are protected either by a supplementary protection certificate under Regulation (EEC) No 1768/92, or by a patent which qualifies for the granting of the supplementary protection certificate, Article 7 of this Regulation shall apply to applications for authorisation of new indications, including paediatric indications, new pharmaceutical forms and new routes of administration.

rules.rule_text: The PIP requirements of Articles 7 and 8 do not apply to products authorised under the generic, well-established use, traditional herbal, homeopathic, or similar simplified procedures of Directive 2001/83/EC (Articles 10, 10a, 13-16, 16a-16i).
rules.verbatim_quote: Articles 7 and 8 shall not apply to products authorised under Articles 10, 10a, 13 to 16 or 16a to 16i of Directive 2001/83/EC.

rules.rule_text: The 'paediatric population' is defined as the part of the population aged between birth and 18 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'paediatric population' means that part of the population aged between birth and 18 years;

rules.rule_text: A 'paediatric investigation plan' (PIP) means a research and development programme aimed at ensuring that the necessary data are generated determining the conditions in which a medicinal product may be authorised to treat the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'paediatric investigation plan' means a research and development programme aimed at ensuring that the necessary data are generated determining the conditions in which a medicinal product may be authorised to treat the paediatric population;

rules.rule_text: A Paediatric Committee shall be established within the European Medicines Agency, with expertise and competence in the development and assessment of all aspects of medicinal products to treat paediatric populations.
rules.verbatim_quote: By 26 July 2007, a Paediatric Committee shall be established within the European Medicines Agency set up under Regulation (EC) No 726/2004, hereinafter 'the Agency'. The Paediatric Committee shall be considered as established once the members referred to in Article 4(1)(a) and (b) have been appointed.

rules.rule_text: Members of the Paediatric Committee shall not have financial or other interests in the pharmaceutical industry which could affect their impartiality, and shall make an annual declaration of their financial interests.
rules.verbatim_quote: Members of the Paediatric Committee should therefore not have financial or other interests in the pharmaceutical industry which could affect their impartiality, should undertake to act in the public interest and in an independent manner, and should make an annual declaration of their financial interests.

rules.rule_text: When carrying out its tasks, the Paediatric Committee shall consider whether or not any proposed studies can be expected to be of significant therapeutic benefit to and/or fulfil a therapeutic need of the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: When carrying out its tasks, the Paediatric Committee shall consider whether or not any proposed studies can be expected to be of significant therapeutic benefit to and/or fulfil a therapeutic need of the paediatric population.

rules.rule_text: The PIP requirement (Article 7(1)(a)) shall be waived for specific medicinal products or classes if there is evidence that: (a) the product is likely to be ineffective or unsafe in part or all of the paediatric population; (b) the disease or condition occurs only in adult populations; (c) the product does not represent a significant therapeutic benefit over existing treatments for paediatric patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: Production of the information referred to in point (a) of Article 7(1) shall be waived for specific medicinal products or for classes of medicinal products, if there is evidence showing any of the following: (a) that the specific medicinal product or class of medicinal products is likely to be ineffective or unsafe in part or all of the paediatric population; (b) that the disease or condition for which the specific medicinal product or class is intended occurs only in adult populations; (c) that the specific medicinal product does not represent a significant therapeutic benefit over existing treatments for paediatric patients.

rules.rule_text: A waiver may be issued with reference either to one or more specified subsets of the paediatric population, or to one or more specified therapeutic indications, or to a combination of both.
rules.verbatim_quote: The waiver provided for in paragraph 1 may be issued with reference either to one or more specified subsets of the paediatric population, or to one or more specified therapeutic indications, or to a combination of both.

rules.rule_text: The Paediatric Committee may of its own motion adopt an opinion that a class or product-specific waiver should be granted (Article 12). The procedure laid down in Article 25 shall apply.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Paediatric Committee may of its own motion adopt an opinion, on the grounds set out in Article 11(1), to the effect that a class or a product-specific waiver, as referred to in Article 11(1), should be granted.

rules.rule_text: The applicant may apply to the Agency for a product-specific waiver on the grounds set out in Article 11(1). The Paediatric Committee shall appoint a rapporteur and within 60 days adopt an opinion as to whether or not a product-specific waiver should be granted.
rules.verbatim_quote: The applicant may, on the grounds set out in Article 11(1), apply to the Agency for a product-specific waiver. ... Following receipt of the application, the Paediatric Committee shall appoint a rapporteur and shall within 60 days adopt an opinion as to whether or not a product-specific waiver should be granted.

rules.rule_text: The Agency shall maintain a list of all waivers. The list shall be regularly updated (at least every year) and made available to the public.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Agency shall maintain a list of all waivers. The list shall be regularly updated (at least every year) and made available to the public.

rules.rule_text: If a product-specific or class waiver is revoked, the requirement set out in Articles 7 and 8 shall not apply for 36 months from the date of the removal from the list of waivers.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a particular product-specific or class waiver is revoked, the requirement set out in Articles 7 and 8 shall not apply for 36 months from the date of the removal from the list of waivers.

rules.rule_text: Where the intention is to apply for a marketing authorisation in accordance with Article 7(1)(a) or (d), Article 8 or Article 30, a paediatric investigation plan shall be drawn up and submitted to the Agency with a request for agreement.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where the intention is to apply for a marketing authorisation in accordance with Article 7(1)(a) or (d), Article 8 or Article 30, a paediatric investigation plan shall be drawn up and submitted to the Agency with a request for agreement.

rules.rule_text: The PIP shall specify the timing and the measures proposed to assess the quality, safety and efficacy of the medicinal product in all subsets of the paediatric population that may be concerned. It shall also describe any measures to adapt the formulation to make its use more acceptable, easier, safer or more effective for different subsets.
rules.verbatim_quote: The paediatric investigation plan shall specify the timing and the measures proposed to assess the quality, safety and efficacy of the medicinal product in all subsets of the paediatric population that may be concerned. In addition, it shall describe any measures to adapt the formulation of the medicinal product so as to make its use more acceptable, easier, safer or more effective for different subsets of the paediatric population.

rules.rule_text: The PIP or application for waiver shall be submitted with a request for agreement not later than upon completion of the human pharmaco-kinetic studies in adults specified in Section 5.2.3 of Part I of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC, except in duly justified cases.
rules.verbatim_quote: the paediatric investigation plan or the application for waiver shall be submitted with a request for agreement, except in duly justified cases, not later than upon completion of the human pharmaco-kinetic studies in adults specified in Section 5.2.3 of Part I of Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC, so as to ensure that an opinion on use in the paediatric population of the medicinal product concerned can be given at the time of the assessment of the marketing authorisation or other application concerned.
cross_references: Directive 2001/83/EC Annex I, Part I, Section 5.2.3

rules.rule_text: The Agency shall verify the validity of the PIP request within 30 days and prepare a summary report for the Paediatric Committee.
rules.verbatim_quote: Within 30 days following receipt of the request referred to in paragraph 1 and in Article 15(1), the Agency shall verify the validity of the request and prepare a summary report for the Paediatric Committee.

rules.rule_text: The Paediatric Committee shall appoint a rapporteur and within 60 days adopt an opinion on whether the proposed PIP will ensure generation of the necessary data for paediatric use, and whether the expected therapeutic benefits justify the studies proposed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Following receipt of a proposed paediatric investigation plan which is valid in accordance with the provisions of Article 15(2), the Paediatric Committee shall appoint a rapporteur and shall within 60 days adopt an opinion as to whether or not the proposed studies will ensure the generation of the necessary data determining the conditions in which the medicinal product may be used to treat the paediatric population or subsets thereof, and as to whether or not the expected therapeutic benefits justify the studies proposed.

rules.rule_text: The Paediatric Committee may request the applicant to propose modifications to the PIP, in which case the 60-day time-limit for the final opinion shall be extended to a maximum of 60 additional days.
rules.verbatim_quote: Within the 60-day period referred to in paragraph 1, the Paediatric Committee may request the applicant to propose modifications to the plan, in which case the time-limit referred to in paragraph 1 for the adoption of the final opinion shall be extended to a maximum of 60 days.

rules.rule_text: If the Paediatric Committee concludes that Article 11(1)(a), (b) or (c) applies to the proposed PIP, it shall adopt a negative opinion on the PIP and instead adopt an opinion in favour of a waiver under Article 12.
rules.verbatim_quote: If, having considered a paediatric investigation plan, the Paediatric Committee concludes that Article 11(1)(a), (b) or (c) applies to the medicinal product concerned, it shall adopt a negative opinion under Article 17(1). In such cases, the Paediatric Committee shall adopt an opinion in favour of a waiver under Article 12, whereupon the procedure laid down in Article 25 shall apply.

rules.rule_text: A deferral of the initiation or completion of some or all of the measures in a PIP may be requested at the same time the PIP is submitted under Article 16(1). Such deferral shall be justified on scientific and technical grounds or on grounds related to public health.
rules.verbatim_quote: At the same time as the paediatric investigation plan is submitted under Article 16(1), a request may be made for deferral of the initiation or completion of some or all of the measures set out in that plan. Such deferral shall be justified on scientific and technical grounds or on grounds related to public health.

rules.rule_text: A deferral shall be granted when it is appropriate to conduct studies in adults prior to initiating studies in the paediatric population or when studies in the paediatric population will take longer to conduct than studies in adults.
rules.verbatim_quote: In any event, a deferral shall be granted when it is appropriate to conduct studies in adults prior to initiating studies in the paediatric population or when studies in the paediatric population will take longer to conduct than studies in adults.

rules.rule_text: If the applicant encounters implementation difficulties rendering the PIP unworkable or no longer appropriate, the applicant may propose changes or request a deferral or waiver, based on detailed grounds, to the Paediatric Committee. The Committee shall review within 60 days.
rules.verbatim_quote: If, following the decision agreeing the paediatric investigation plan, the applicant encounters such difficulties with its implementation as to render the plan unworkable or no longer appropriate, the applicant may propose changes or request a deferral or a waiver, based on detailed grounds, to the Paediatric Committee. Within 60 days, the Paediatric Committee shall review these changes or the request for a deferral or a waiver and adopt an opinion proposing their refusal or acceptance.

rules.rule_text: The competent authority responsible for granting marketing authorisation shall verify whether an application for marketing authorisation or variation complies with Articles 7 and 8 and whether an application submitted pursuant to Article 30 complies with the agreed PIP.
rules.verbatim_quote: The competent authority responsible for granting marketing authorisation shall verify whether an application for marketing authorisation or variation complies with the requirements laid down in Articles 7 and 8 and whether an application submitted pursuant to Article 30 complies with the agreed paediatric investigation plan.

rules.rule_text: If paediatric studies are not in conformity with the agreed PIP, the product shall not be eligible for rewards and incentives provided for in Articles 36, 37 and 38.
rules.verbatim_quote: If, when conducting the scientific assessment of a valid application for Marketing Authorisation, the competent authority concludes that the studies are not in conformity with the agreed paediatric investigation plan, the product shall not be eligible for the rewards and incentives provided for in Articles 36, 37 and 38.

rules.rule_text: Within ten days of receipt, the Agency shall transmit the opinion of the Paediatric Committee to the applicant.
rules.verbatim_quote: Within ten days of its receipt, the Agency shall transmit the opinion of the Paediatric Committee to the applicant.

rules.rule_text: The applicant may submit a written request for re-examination of the Paediatric Committee opinion within 30 days of receipt, citing detailed grounds. A new rapporteur shall be appointed and shall issue a new opinion within 30 days.
rules.verbatim_quote: Within 30 days following receipt of the opinion of the Paediatric Committee, the applicant may submit to the Agency a written request, citing detailed grounds, for a re-examination of the opinion. ... Within 30 days following receipt of a request for re-examination pursuant to paragraph 2, the Paediatric Committee, having appointed a new rapporteur, shall issue a new opinion confirming or revising its previous opinion.

rules.rule_text: The Agency shall adopt a decision within 10 days following receipt of the Paediatric Committee's definitive opinion, communicated in writing to the applicant with the definitive opinion annexed. Decisions shall be made public after deletion of commercially confidential information.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Agency shall adopt a decision within a period not exceeding 10 days following receipt of the Paediatric Committee's definitive opinion. This decision shall be communicated to the applicant in writing and shall annex the definitive opinion of the Paediatric Committee. ... Decisions of the Agency shall be made public after deletion of any information of a commercially confidential nature.

rules.rule_text: Any person developing a medicinal product intended for paediatric use may, prior to the submission of a PIP and during its implementation, request free scientific advice from the Agency on the design and conduct of studies to demonstrate quality, safety, and efficacy in the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any legal or natural person developing a medicinal product intended for paediatric use may, prior to the submission of a paediatric investigation plan and during its implementation, request advice from the Agency on the design and conduct of the various tests and studies necessary to demonstrate the quality, safety and efficacy of the medicinal product in the paediatric population in accordance with Article 57(1)(n) of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004. ... The Agency shall provide advice under this Article free of charge.

rules.rule_text: Where authorisation is granted for a product including paediatric indications based on an agreed PIP, the results of all those studies shall be included in the SmPC and, if appropriate, in the package leaflet.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where authorisation is granted, the results of all those studies shall be included in the summary of product characteristics and, if appropriate, in the package leaflet of the medicinal product, provided that the competent authority deems the information to be of use to patients, whether or not all the paediatric indications concerned were approved by the competent authority.

rules.rule_text: Where a marketing authorisation is granted or varied, any waiver or deferral which has been granted shall be recorded in the SmPC and, if appropriate, in the package leaflet of the medicinal product concerned.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where a marketing authorisation is granted or varied, any waiver or deferral which has been granted pursuant to this Regulation shall be recorded in the summary of product characteristics and, if appropriate, in the package leaflet of the medicinal product concerned.

rules.rule_text: If the application complies with all measures in the agreed completed PIP and if the SmPC reflects the results of studies conducted in compliance with that PIP, the competent authority shall include a statement of compliance in the marketing authorisation.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the application complies with all the measures contained in the agreed completed paediatric investigation plan and if the summary of product characteristics reflects the results of studies conducted in compliance with that agreed paediatric investigation plan, the competent authority shall include within the marketing authorisation a statement indicating compliance of the application with the agreed completed paediatric investigation plan.

rules.rule_text: An application for a paediatric use marketing authorisation (PUMA) shall be accompanied by the particulars and documents necessary to establish quality, safety and efficacy in the paediatric population, including any specific data needed to support an appropriate strength, pharmaceutical form or route of administration, in accordance with an agreed PIP.
rules.verbatim_quote: An application for a paediatric use marketing authorisation shall be accompanied by the particulars and documents necessary to establish quality, safety and efficacy in the paediatric population, including any specific data needed to support an appropriate strength, pharmaceutical form or route of administration for the product, in accordance with an agreed paediatric investigation plan.

rules.rule_text: Where a marketing authorisation is granted for a paediatric indication, the label shall display a paediatric symbol selected by the Commission following a recommendation by the Paediatric Committee. The package leaflet shall contain an explanation of the meaning of the symbol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where a marketing authorisation is granted for a paediatric indication, the label shall display the symbol agreed in accordance with paragraph 2. The package leaflet shall contain an explanation of the meaning of the symbol. ... By 26 January 2008, the Commission shall select a symbol following a recommendation of the Paediatric Committee. The Commission shall make the symbol public.

rules.rule_text: Where a medicinal product is authorised for a paediatric indication following completion of an agreed PIP and those products have already been marketed for other indications, the MAH shall place the product on the market taking into account the paediatric indication within two years of the date of approval.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where medicinal products are authorised for a paediatric indication following completion of an agreed paediatric investigation plan and those products have already been marketed with other indications, the marketing authorisation holder shall, within two years of the date on which the paediatric indication is authorised, place the product on the market taking into account the paediatric indication.

rules.rule_text: For applications including a paediatric indication, the applicant shall detail the measures to ensure the follow-up of efficacy and of possible adverse reactions to the paediatric use of the medicinal product. Where there is particular cause for concern, the competent authority shall require a risk management system be set up or specific post-marketing studies be performed.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the following cases, the applicant shall detail the measures to ensure the follow-up of efficacy and of possible adverse reactions to the paediatric use of the medicinal product: (a) applications for a marketing authorisation that includes a paediatric indication; (b) applications to include a paediatric indication in an existing marketing authorisation; (c) applications for a paediatric use marketing authorisation. ... Where there is particular cause for concern, the competent authority shall require, as a condition for granting marketing authorisation, that a risk management system be set up or that specific post-marketing studies be performed and submitted for review.

rules.rule_text: In the case of a deferral, the MAH shall submit an annual report to the Agency providing an update on progress with paediatric studies in accordance with the decision of the Agency agreeing the PIP and granting a deferral.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the case of a deferral, the marketing authorisation holder shall submit an annual report to the Agency providing an update on progress with paediatric studies in accordance with the decision of the Agency agreeing the paediatric investigation plan and granting a deferral.

rules.rule_text: If a MAH intends to discontinue placing the medicinal product on the market after having benefited from rewards or incentives under Articles 36, 37 or 38, the MAH shall transfer the marketing authorisation or allow a third party to use the pharmaceutical, pre-clinical and clinical documentation. The MAH shall inform the Agency of its intention no less than six months before discontinuation.
rules.verbatim_quote: if the marketing authorisation holder intends to discontinue placing the medicinal product on the market, the marketing authorisation holder shall transfer the marketing authorisation or allow a third party, which has declared its intention to continue to place the medicinal product in question on the market, to use the pharmaceutical, pre-clinical and clinical documentation contained in the file of the medicinal product ... The marketing authorisation holder shall inform the Agency of its intention to discontinue the placing on the market of the product no less than six months before the discontinuation. The Agency shall make this fact public.

rules.rule_text: Where an application under Article 7 or 8 includes the results of all studies conducted in compliance with an agreed PIP, the holder of the patent or SPC shall be entitled to a 6-month extension of the SPC period referred to in Regulation (EEC) No 1768/92.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where an application under Article 7 or 8 includes the results of all studies conducted in compliance with an agreed paediatric investigation plan, the holder of the patent or supplementary protection certificate shall be entitled to a six-month extension of the period referred to in Articles 13(1) and 13(2) of Regulation (EEC) No 1768/92.
exceptions: Not applicable to orphan medicinal products designated under Regulation (EC) No 141/2000 (Article 36(4))

rules.rule_text: For orphan medicinal products designated under Regulation (EC) No 141/2000, instead of an SPC extension, the ten-year period of orphan market exclusivity under Article 8(1) of that Regulation shall be extended to twelve years if the requirement for paediatric data on use in the paediatric population is fully met.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where an application for a marketing authorisation is submitted in respect of a medicinal product designated as an orphan medicinal product pursuant to Regulation (EC) No 141/2000 and that application includes the results of all studies conducted in compliance with an agreed paediatric investigation plan, and the statement referred to in Article 28(3) of this Regulation is subsequently included in the marketing authorisation granted, the ten-year period referred to in Article 8(1) of Regulation (EC) No 141/2000 shall be extended to twelve years.

rules.rule_text: Where a paediatric use marketing authorisation (PUMA) is granted in accordance with Articles 5 to 15 of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004, the data and marketing protection periods referred to in Article 14(11) of that Regulation shall apply.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where a paediatric use marketing authorisation is granted in accordance with Articles 5 to 15 of Regulation (EC) No 726/2004, the data and marketing protection periods referred to in Article 14(11) of that Regulation shall apply.

rules.rule_text: All marketing authorisation holder-sponsored studies which involve the use in the paediatric population of a medicinal product covered by a marketing authorisation, whether or not conducted in compliance with an agreed PIP, shall be submitted to the competent authority within six months of completion of the studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any other marketing authorisation holder-sponsored studies which involve the use in the paediatric population of a medicinal product covered by a marketing authorisation, whether or not they are conducted in compliance with an agreed paediatric investigation plan, shall be submitted to the competent authority within six months of completion of the studies concerned.

rules.rule_text: Assessments by the Paediatric Committee of applications for waiver, applications for deferral, paediatric investigation plans, and compliance with the agreed PIP shall be free of charge.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessments of the following by the Paediatric Committee shall be free of charge: (a) applications for waiver; (b) applications for deferral; (c) paediatric investigation plans; (d) compliance with the agreed paediatric investigation plan.
